title: Palicourea nematostachya
taxonomy: Palicourea nematostachya is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Palicourea is native to tropical regions of the Americas and includes about 300 species of shrubs and small trees. The species P. nematostachya is a shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is part of the Rubiales order and the Magnoliophyta class.
uses: Palicourea nematostachya, a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
